sanskrit_devanagari:
अर्जुन उवाच ।
योऽयं योगस्त्वया प्रोक्तः साम्येन मधुसूदन ।
एतस्याहं न पश्यामि चञ्चलत्वात्स्थितिं स्थिराम् ॥ ३३ ॥
sanskrit_iast:
arjuna uvāca |
yo 'yaṁ yogas tvayā proktaḥ sāmyena madhusūdana |
etasyāhaṁ na paśyāmi cañcalatvāt sthitiṁ sthirām || 33 ||

translations.verbatim_quote: Arjuna said: O Madhusūdana, the system of yoga which You have summarized appears impractical and unendurable to me, for the mind is restless and unsteady.
Arjun said: The system of Yog that you have described, O Madhusudan, appears impractical and unattainable to me, due to the restless mind.

commentaries.verbatim_excerpt_status: captured (fair-use excerpt; full purport length: 1716 chars)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: The system of mysticism described by Lord Kṛṣṇa to Arjuna beginning with the words śucau deśe and ending with yogī paramaḥ is here being rejected by Arjuna out of a feeling of inability. It is not possible for an ordinary man to leave home and go to a secluded place in the mountains or jungles to practice yoga in this

commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-6-33-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Ramanuja of Sri Sampradaya:)

positions.rendering: the system of yoga which You have summarized / equanimous in all
yoga taught in the form of equanimity, referring to 6.29-6.32
yoga described as samadarshana — same-seeing of self in all beings

disagreements_among_translators.explanation: All three readings agree that sāmyena refers back to the equanimity-and-same-seeing material of 6.29-6.32. The operational reach is shared: the speaker names the practical impossibility of maintaining the equanimity-state.

literal_meaning: The speaker said: this yoga of equanimity that you have spoken of, O Madhusūdana — its firm continuation I do not see, because of restlessness.
traditional_meaning_consensus: 6.33 is the dialectical break of chapter 6. The student interrupts after the long meditation-instruction (6.10-6.32) and names the practical difficulty. The 'sāmyena' refers back to 6.29-6.32's same-seeing equanimity-yoga. The claim is honest: he does not see how this state can be maintained sthirām (firmly) given the cañcalatva (restlessness) of the mind he is about to describe in 6.34. Sankara's reading: he sees no firm position of this yoga taught in the form of equanimity, because of the restlessness of the mind. Ramanuja's reading: he does not perceive the steady continuance of this yoga which has been described as samadarshana, because of the natural restlessness of the mind. Both traditions read this verse as pre-figuring the explicit restless-mind question of 6.34.
